summary: This week's research on small UAS and FPV drones highlights significant advances in detection, tracking, and counter-UAS technologies. Several papers focus on improving real-time detection of small drones using lightweight neural network architectures (YolovN-CBi, YOLOv11 with multi-scale processing) and novel sensor fusion approaches (WAVE-DETR combining RGB and acoustic signals, neuromorphic cameras with spiking neural networks). Embedded and edge-based tracking frameworks (SDG-Track) address the resolution-speed tradeoff for high-resolution UAV tracking. Counter-UAS efforts include a multi-physics simulation framework for high-power microwave neutralization and evaluations of commercial detection, tracking, and identification (DTI) solutions. Additionally, there is progress in autonomous visual servoing for quadrotor control and integrating counter-UAS systems into UTM for reliable decision-making. These developments reflect a strong emphasis on real-time, robust, and resource-efficient methods for both offensive and defensive small UAS operations.
